what_it_does: Posts a second-pass evidence note that catalogs registry/type families discovered from a field-index and flags a tension about whether the registry mechanism is split between file-discovery and in-code tables.
observations: Seven registry/type families are listed with file counts and example files: role (32), decision (25), mark_type (13), projection (12), flow (8), item_type (7), skill (6).
The note is explicitly framed as non-opinionated evidence, not a conclusion.
A tension section asks Tim to judge whether the split between file-discovered registries and provider/model/capability/address-scheme registries declared elsewhere is intended or convergeable.
It references other registry locations by name: suite.py tables and contracts/address.py.
It links back to a source noticeboard item (item-4f33d628).
standouts: The central tension: registry mechanism appears split across file-discovered families and declared in-code tables.
The role family is the largest with 32 associated files, followed by decision with 25.
The author deliberately separates evidence from judgement.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard YAML frontmatter schema with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history.
Uses a bullet list for enumerated families.
Labels interpretive content as a tension rather than a conclusion.
Cites a prior board item as the source.
concerns: The tension depends on external files (suite.py, contracts/address.py) that are not shown here.
The criteria used to assign files to registry/type families are not explained.
The note leaves an unresolved architectural question without proposing a resolution.
notes: A noticeboard evidence note mapping file-discovered registry/type families and raising an unresolved architectural tension about split registry declaration.
questions: What is the field-index and how were files assigned to each family?
Where exactly are the provider/model, capability, and address-scheme registries defined?
What would convergence look like, and what criteria would decide it?
Is Tim a specific decision owner or a role?
What evidence did the source item (item-4f33d628) contain?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the stated purpose is a registry/type dragnet evidence note, and the file delivers an enumerated list of discovered families plus a tension for judgement.
intention_signals: To consolidate empirical file-name patterns into registry/type families and surface a meta-design question about whether registry mechanisms should be unified.
novelty: It treats filenames as empirical registry/type evidence and contrasts them with explicit in-code registry tables, framing a structural convergence question rather than a technical implementation.
contribution: A neutral empirical inventory of file-derived registry/type families and a clearly scoped architectural tension that a fused system could use when reconciling discovery-based and declaration-based registries.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard note item-16cd0303 catalogs seven file-discovered registry/type families with counts and example files, then flags a tension that provider/model, capability, and address-scheme registries are declared in code tables elsewhere.
intention_for_embedding: To present second-pass evidence from a registry/type dragnet and invite judgement on whether the split between file-discovered and in-code registry mechanisms is intended or convergeable.
